name: Las Chorreras – circular desde Mecina Bombarón — Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
region_intro: Hiking around Alpujarra De La Sierra offers diverse terrain on the southern slopes of Spain's Sierra Nevada mountains. The region features picturesque valleys, dramatic mountain landscapes, and varied ecosystems including scrubland, extensive forests of holm oaks, pine, and chestnut trees, alongside terraced olive and almond groves. Notable features include the Poqueira Gorge, carved by its namesake river, and numerous natural springs. Best hiking trails around Alpujarra De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Health Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This moderate loop offers expansive views from Yegen. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mecina Bombarón, Yegen, and Golco Circular, a difficult 10.9 miles (17.7 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes, including traditional villages and open countryside. Local hikers also love the Bérchules – Acequia Nueva loop from Bérchules, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through ancient irrigation channels, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Alpujarra De La Sierra is defined by picturesque valleys, dramatic mountain landscapes, and ancient irrigation canals. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging ascents. The routes in Alpujarra De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Alpujarra De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: Alpujarra De La Sierra offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 70 routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: The region is characterized by picturesque valleys and dramatic mountain landscapes, offering panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada. You'll traverse varied terrains, including scrubland, lush river valleys, extensive forests of holm oaks, pine, and chestnut trees, alongside terraced olive and almond groves. The iconic Poqueira Gorge is a notable feature, as are numerous natural springs and ancient irrigation canals.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: Yes, there are several easier routes suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981467895/health-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Health Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate loop offering expansive views from Yegen, which can be a good option for those looking for a less strenuous walk.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I might encounter on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore the famous 'Pueblos Blancos' (white villages) like Pampaneira, Bubión, and Capileira, known for their traditional Moorish architecture. Natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñabón&lt;/a&gt; summit, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3866790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benéjar River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4418355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Rivers (Cruce de Los Ríos) – El Horcajo&lt;/a&gt;. The region's ancient irrigation canals, known as 'acequias,' also offer a unique blend of historical insight and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981454206/mecina-bombaron-yegen-and-golco-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mecina Bombarón, Yegen, and Golco Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which traverses varied landscapes and traditional villages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for hiking. In spring, the gorse and broom burst into bloom, painting the landscape with vibrant yellow flowers. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall colors. While summer can be hot, higher altitude trails provide cooler conditions. Winter hiking is possible, especially at lower elevations, but snow can be present on higher peaks.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while hiking in the Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: The region is rich in biodiversity. Hikers often have opportunities to spot the iconic Spanish ibex on rocky slopes. Griffon vultures and golden eagles can frequently be seen soaring above the mountains.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: The routes in Alpujarra De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, stunning views, and the unique cultural experience of passing through traditional white villages and along ancient acequias.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the ancient irrigation channels (acequias)?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its ancient Moorish irrigation canals, or 'acequias,' which are still in use today. Many trails follow these peaceful waterways, offering unique historical insight and panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bérchules – Acequia Nueva loop from Bérchules&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that leads through these channels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the hiking trails in Alpujarra De La Sierra?
A: The trails in Alpujarra De La Sierra cater to a range of abilities. You'll find easy strolls, moderate paths, and challenging ascents. Approximately 17 routes are considered easy, 36 are moderate, and 23 are difficult, ensuring options for every experience level.

Q: Is public transport or parking readily available for accessing trailheads?
A: While specific public transport options vary by village, many of the white villages are connected by local bus services, which can be useful for point-to-point hikes. Parking is generally available in or near the villages that serve as trailheads, though it can be limited in smaller, more traditional settlements. It's advisable to check local information for specific parking availability.
tips: The Las Chorreras waterfall is beautiful. A place that emanates tranquility in the middle of nature.
This viewpoint offers an excellent panoramic view of the valley with views of the towns of Narila and Cádiar.
